title: capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. orientalis
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. orientalis, commonly known as oriental shepherd's purse, is a subspecies of the plant Capsella bursa-pastoris, a member of the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is often found in fields and along roadsides. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds. The health benefits of oriental shepherd's purse include its ability to reduce bleeding and swelling, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve digestive health. Oriental shepherd's purse can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
